name: Line Divide POP
description: The Line Divide POP takes any set of line strips, and for each it generates a new line strip that is subdivided using various interpolation methods. The default is linear interpolation with 4 divisions between incoming points. You can choose the Interpolation Method, the default being Linear interpolation, but you can also choose spline types like Cardinal, BSpline, Beziers and Quadratic. With the spline types, it can use incoming attributes (like those set in the [[Line POP]]) that specify the spline properties like Weight, tangents (TanIn and TanOut), tangent constraints (TanInConstraint) and/or tension. It will also interpret an interpolation method per-point if a SegMethod attribute exists. There are two stages of doing divisions, The first stage allows setting the total divisions for the line strip, or divisions per-segment, dividing by distance along the curves, or by curvature of the line strip. It interpolates all the attributes. The second stage lets you further re-sample the line strip with one of three methods: Divisions per Line Strip, Distance between Points, or Points as Keyframes. Points as Keyframes interpolates by treating one attribute as an independent variable (like Time, or U), like a y = f(x) mathematical function. (For example, if you have an attribute Time on the points, you can resample curves at equal steps of time, assuming you curves express values over time.) NOTE: The Maximum Number of Vertices parameter is required because it is not known on the GPU how many points you will end up with, and the GPU needs to pre-allocate enough memory to do the job. Oherwise line strips will be truncated. To add: distinguish the different beziers, explain TanInConstraint. ControlPoint attribute (laser) See also [[Line POP]], [[Curve POP]], [[Point POP]], [[Primitive POP]], [[Pattern POP]].

parameters.name: Divisions Method
parameters.label: Divisions Method
parameters.description: The method to apply the divisions.

parameters.name: Divisions
parameters.label: Divisions
parameters.description: The number of divisions.

parameters.name: Min Distance
parameters.label: Min Distance
parameters.description: The minimum distance between points in the output line strips.

parameters.name: Max Distance
parameters.label: Max Distance
parameters.description: Divide the line so there are no two adjacent points farther apart than this distance - max distance between points.

parameters.name: Min Max Bias
parameters.label: Min Max Bias
parameters.description: Bias of point creation - more near the minimum or more near the maximum distance.

parameters.name: By Edge
parameters.label: By Edge
parameters.description: When dividing using the distance between points, whether to restart at each segment (edge).

parameters.name: Add ControlPoint Attribute
parameters.label: Add ControlPoint Attribute
parameters.description: Whether to output a ControlPoint attribute set to 1 for control points.

parameters.name: Maximum Number of Vertices
parameters.label: Maximum Number of Vertices
parameters.description: Sets the number of vertices to be allocated. It should be bigger than the actual number of vertices created (visible in info popup). More vertices allocated use more GPU memory.

parameters.name: Interpolation Method per Segment
parameters.label: Interpolation Method per Segment
parameters.description: When toggle is on, it uses a separate interpolation method for each segment in the input line strips.

parameters.name: Interpolation Method
parameters.label: Interpolation Method
parameters.description: Method to use for interpolation.

parameters.name: Weight Attribute
parameters.label: Weight Attribute
parameters.description: When on, the points will have a weight attribute affecting the tightness of the curve around the point.

parameters.name: In Tangent Attribute
parameters.label: In Tangent Attribute
parameters.description: When on, the points will have a attribute to be the tangent going into the point.

parameters.name: Out Tangent Attribute
parameters.label: Out Tangent Attribute
parameters.description: When on, the points will have a attribute to be the tangent going out of the point.

parameters.name: In Tangent Constraint Attribute
parameters.label: In Tangent Constraint Attribute
parameters.description: When on, the points will have an attribute to be constraints of a curve at that point..

parameters.name: Clamped
parameters.label: Clamped
parameters.description: When using Cardinal or BSpline interpolation, whether to duplicate the start and end control points so the resulting curve start and end at the control points. Only has an effect on open line strips.

parameters.name: Tension
parameters.label: Tension
parameters.description: Controls how tightly the cardinal line strip bends through the control points

parameters.name: Post-Resample Method
parameters.label: Post-Resample Method
parameters.description: Resampling method used after division.

parameters.name: Divisions
parameters.label: Divisions
parameters.description: The number of divisions.

parameters.name: Independant Variable Attribute
parameters.label: Independant Variable Attribute
parameters.description: Attribute to use as the independent variable.

parameters.name: Independant Variable Step
parameters.label: Independant Variable Step
parameters.description: Step size to use for the independent variable, usually a fraction of the independent variable range.

parameters.name: Maximum Number of Vertices
parameters.label: Maximum Number of Vertices
parameters.description: Sets the number of vertices to be allocated. It should be bigger than the actual number of vertices created (visible in info popup). More vertices allocated use more GPU memory.

parameters.name: Max Tries for Binary Search
parameters.label: Max Tries for Binary Search
parameters.description: Max number of iterations for binary search when linearly resampling.

parameters.name: Remove Unused Points
parameters.label: Remove Unused Points
parameters.description: Removes unused points not referenced by primitives.

parameters.name: Max Distance
parameters.label: Max Distance
parameters.description: Maximum Distance between Points
